chapter_title: Residues and waste from the food industries; prepared animal fodder
inclusions: Flours, meals and pellets of meat or meat offal, of fish, crustaceans, molluscs or other aquatic invertebrates — unfit for human consumption; greaves (rendered fat residues from meat processing)
Bran, sharps and other residues from sifting, milling or other working of cereals (maize, wheat, rice, other cereals) or leguminous plants — whether or not in pellet form
Residues of starch manufacture (including from manioc/cassava, sago); beet-pulp, bagasse and other waste of sugar manufacture; brewing or distilling dregs and waste
Oil-cake and other solid residues from extraction of soya-bean oil (including defatted soya flour and soya meal fit for human consumption)
Oil-cake and other solid residues from extraction of ground-nut (peanut) oil
Oil-cake and other solid residues from extraction of other vegetable or microbial fats or oils (cotton, linseed, sunflower, rape/colza, coconut/copra, palm nut/kernel, maize germ, other)
Wine lees; argol (crude potassium tartrate from wine)
Vegetable materials and vegetable waste, vegetable residues and by-products of a kind used in animal feeding, not elsewhere specified or included
Preparations of a kind used in animal feeding: dog or cat food put up for retail sale; complete feed for livestock and aquaculture; premixes, feed supplements and feed additives

exclusions.condition: Fish flour, meat flour or similar products that are fit for human consumption
exclusions.action: Redirect to appropriate food chapter (Chapter 3, 16, or 11) — Chapter 23 covers only items UNFIT for human consumption (except special cases in 23.04 for defatted soya flour)

exclusions.condition: Vegetable waste, vegetable residues and by-products from processing that retain characteristics of original material — these go to 23.08, NOT 23.09
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 23.08 (vegetable waste NESOI for animal feeding)

exclusions.condition: Oil-cake from soya-bean oil extraction → heading 23.04 (not 23.06)
Oil-cake from ground-nut (peanut) oil extraction → heading 23.05 (not 23.06)

classification_rules.rule: Heading 23.09 scope — processed animal feed that has lost original material characteristics
classification_rules.description: Heading 23.09 covers preparations used in animal feeding, NOT elsewhere specified, obtained by processing vegetable or animal materials to the extent that they have LOST THE ESSENTIAL CHARACTERISTICS of the original material. Key: significant processing must have occurred. HOWEVER, vegetable waste, vegetable residues and by-products of such processing do NOT fall into 23.09 — they go into 23.08 instead.

classification_rules.rule: Fish flour protein content determines sub-classification within 2301.20
classification_rules.description: Fish flours, meals and pellets under heading 2301.20 are split by protein content: 2301.20.10 = protein content LESS THAN 60% by weight; 2301.20.20 = protein content OF 60% OR MORE by weight. This threshold (60%) is the critical subheading distinction within fish meal.

Oil-cake classification follows a strict hierarchy: (1) From soya-bean oil → 23.04; (2) From ground-nut oil → 23.05; (3) From ALL OTHER vegetable/microbial oils (cotton, linseed, sunflower, rape/colza, coconut, palm, maize germ, etc.) → 23.06. The 'other' category 23.06 explicitly excludes headings 23.04 and 23.05.

classification_rules.rule: Soya-bean products in 23.04 may be fit for human consumption
classification_rules.description: Unlike most of Chapter 23 (residues unfit for humans), heading 23.04 can include: defatted soya-bean flour fit for human consumption (2304.00.10) and soya-bean meal fit for human consumption (2304.00.21). These are classified in Chapter 23 because they are residues from oil extraction, even though edible.

classification_rules.rule: Low erucic acid rape/colza seed definition for 2306.41
classification_rules.description: The expression 'low erucic acid rape or colza seeds' for subheading 2306.41 refers to seeds as defined in Subheading Note 1 to Chapter 12. This definition must be cross-referenced to Chapter 12 subheading notes to determine whether the seeds qualify as 'low erucic acid'.

classification_rules.rule: 2302 covers milling residues from cereals AND leguminous plants
classification_rules.description: Heading 23.02 covers bran, sharps and similar residues from sifting, milling or other working of BOTH cereals (maize, wheat, rice, other cereals) AND leguminous plants. Residues from leguminous plants (peas, beans, lentils, etc.) → 2302.50.

Dog or cat food PUT UP FOR RETAIL SALE → 2309.10 regardless of composition. If containing meat → 2309.10.10; other (grain-based, vegetarian) → 2309.10.90. Industrial/bulk animal feed for all species (poultry, swine, fish, primates, etc.) falls under 2309.90.
